details.summary: STT server and backend proxy implemented correctly, but route tests duplicate logic inline instead of testing actual stt.ts module, and server.ts lacks express.raw() middleware for audio content types.
TTS server meets all expected behaviors: runs on port 8766, synthesize returns MP3, backend proxy works. Three non-blocking issues noted (request size limit, retry logic, input validation).

Blocking: useState used for cleanup instead of useEffect (mic stays active, event listeners leak). setStatus('permission-denied') overwritten to 'error' by setError, preventing permission-denied UI from rendering.

blockingIssues.description: Route tests (backend/routes/stt.test.ts) define inline route handlers that duplicate the routing logic instead of importing and testing the actual stt.ts module. Real route code changes are unvalidated.
blockingIssues.suggestedFix: Import sttRoutes from stt.ts and mount with app.use('/api/stt', sttRoutes) with appropriate middleware (express.raw({ type: 'audio/*' })) configured.

blockingIssues.description: server.ts does not configure express.raw() middleware for STT routes. The STT route falls back to manually reading the request stream which is fragile and not robust.
blockingIssues.suggestedFix: Add express.raw({ type: 'audio/*', limit: '50mb' }) middleware configured for the /api/stt route path.

blockingIssues.description: VoiceControls.tsx:197 uses useState(() => { ... return () => cleanup }) to register cleanup on unmount. useState never calls the returned function as cleanup — the cleanup value is stored as state instead. Microphone stays active and audio resources leak on unmount.
blockingIssues.suggestedFix: Replace useState with useEffect that returns the cleanup function.

blockingIssues.description: VoiceOutputBridge (VoiceControls.tsx:379) uses useState to register an event listener with cleanup. The removeEventListener is never called on unmount, causing memory leak and stale closures.
blockingIssues.suggestedFix: Replace useState with useEffect for the event listener registration and cleanup.

blockingIssues.description: voiceStore.ts:95: setError unconditionally sets status to 'error' when error is truthy. When VoiceInputManager calls setStatus('permission-denied') then setError(), the status is overwritten to 'error', making permission-denied UI unreachable.
blockingIssues.suggestedFix: Either preserve 'permission-denied' status in setError, or prevent setError from being called after permission-denied status is set, or use a separate mechanism for permission-denied handling.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add coding convention: 'Always use useEffect for cleanup functions on unmount, never useState. useState only stores return values as state, it does not call the returned function as cleanup.'
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: voice-ui-controls: VoiceControls.tsx uses useState(() => { return () => cleanup }) at lines 197 and 379 — cleanup never executes.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add pitfall documentation: 'When using setError in a store that also manages status, be aware that setStatus transitions may be overwritten. For specific status values like permission-denied, either use setStatus without setError, or modify setError to preserve specific status values.'
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: voice-ui-controls: voiceStore.ts setError overwrites 'permission-denied' to 'error', preventing dedicated UI.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Update port range guidance to clarify that STT (8765) and TTS (8766) servers are approved exceptions to the 3100-3199 range, or expand the allowed range to include these ports.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: AGENTS.md states 3100-3199 but voice milestone uses 8765/8766. Contradiction may confuse future workers.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Replace hardcoded absolute paths for STT and TTS services with relative paths from repo root.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: STT service: 'cd /Users/stefano/repos/open-marcus/servers/stt && node server.mjs --port 8765'. TTS service: similar pattern. These will break on any other machine or CI.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add setup documentation: 'Servers in subdirectories (e.g., servers/stt/, servers/tts/) require their own npm install / pip install steps. Add these to services.yaml or as project scripts so they run automatically.'
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: servers/stt/package.json requires sherpa-onnx-node, but no npm install step exists for this subdirectory. STT server also references a download-model.sh script that doesn't exist.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add coding rule: 'Every component that creates resources on mount (event listeners, media streams, intervals, subscriptions) must clean them up in a useEffect return function. This includes Web Audio API contexts, MediaRecorder, Audio elements, and WebSocket connections.'
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: voice-ui-controls: VoiceInputManager creates MediaRecorder and Web Audio API connections but cleanup never runs.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add guidance to backend-worker skill for handling TDD with separate-process integrations (e.g., external servers that cannot be unit-tested with Vitest mocking). Clarify when manual testing vs automated testing is acceptable.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: STT worker deviated from TDD — implemented server.mjs and curl-tested manually before writing TypeScript tests, justified because standalone server cannot be tested with Vitest.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add guidance to frontend-worker skill about handling react-refresh/only-export-components ESLint rule when files need to export both React components and utility functions. Consider recommending extracting utilities to a separate module.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: voice-ui-controls: VoiceControls.tsx uses eslint-disable to bypass the rule for dispatchSpeak utility export.

rejectedObservations.observation: RecordingIndicator component is dead code (never imported)
